session_note: S103 - TD Defenders Bundle: #8 Voltkin-vs-chewer + #9 Laser Turret + #10 HELGA princess, on a generic Defender substrate built FIRST (findNearestEnemyCreature + lightning-cloud + recipe generalization). Then Tier-1 G4 juice (budget-gated). Owner: 'run the bundle ... build the generic Defender substrate + findNearestEnemyCreature + lightning-cloud FIRST ... and any other top recommended priorities. i pre approve full session and autonomous run' = unlock_source:user. FULL tier; 3-way Council (Grok REWORK + Gemini ADOPT-WITH-FIXES -> SYNTHESIS ADOPT-WITH-FIXES, 8 must-fixes folded) + PRIME-AUDIT. PDR: .claude/plans/2026-06-25_PDR_Session_103_TD_Defenders_Bundle.md
council_result: 1-round 3-way Council (Claude + Grok-ANALYST grok-4.20-reasoning + Gemini-AUDITOR gemini-2.5-pro) on the S103 TD-defenders batch PDR. Grok: REWORK (Voltkin-death watcher unreliable @10Hz; Voltkin target priority backwards; replay re-baseline violates HARD gate; serialization/host-only stripping underspecified; P2 clones not generalizes). Gemini: ADOPT-WITH-FIXES (transient VFX triggers incompatible with 10Hz full-snapshot -> use synced STATE; insta-fire-on-load bug; sync visually-relevant state; protocol scope; ENDORSES HELGA puppet rig + recipe-break removal). SYNTHESIS=ADOPT-WITH-FIXES (foundations-first sequencing endorsed by both). 8 must-fixes folded: MF1 attack VFX (laser beam/HELGA slap) STATE-derived from a FIRE/SLAP FSM state held ~12 ticks + SYNCED (not cadence-derived); MF2 death VFX KEEP render-driven prev/curr diff (EVIDENCE-BASED PARTIAL OVERRULE - the proven S102 zero-wire pattern; DESPAWNING lasts ~60 ticks so the kill-vs-despawn discriminator is robust; fallback=synced recentDeaths[] if playtest shows misses); MF3 Voltkin BONDS-primary, creatures OPPORTUNISTIC (zap only if already within attackRange 180, never navigate toward distant chewers); MF4 two-seed determinism gate stays GREEN (never re-baselined) - only golden behavior-snapshots for chewer-present scenarios get new baselines, each independently 2-seed verified; Voltkin-ONLY replays stay byte-identical by construction; MF5 insta-fire-on-load fix (re-phase nextFireTick on load); MF6 sorted-by-id serialization + ALL defender fields synced (beam/windup need them), Creature.targetCreatureId host-only stripped (no creature-reticle) -> P1 no bump, P2 PROTOCOL 11->12 for the defenders map; MF7 generalize-not-clone (one findNearestEnemyCreatureFrom + one ignition helper + register orphaned PENTAGRAM_RECIPE); MF8 Defender.hp + damageDefender stub (future lever, no re-bump) + expanded runDefenderStress (save/load insta-fire, co-resident defenders, recipe-break mid-fight, unordered combo, Voltkin target-switch). PRIME-AUDIT: boot-then-smoke mandatory (Voltkin opportunistic zap; turret beam+windup+kill visible to 1v1 joiner; HELGA slap; Voltkin->cloud on 2 hits); determinism gate per priority; HELGA animation-bar honesty (distinct authored poses per state, real windup->impact->recover slap, NOT a twitch/loop).

priorities.label: S103 P1 - Creature-combat foundation + #8 Voltkin-vs-chewer: generic findNearestEnemyCreatureFrom (lowest-id, range-gated) + Creature.targetCreatureId (host-only); Voltkin zaps an enemy chewer ONLY when already within attackRange (bonds stay primary nav); creature-target branch in applyCreatureAttack -> damageCreature(1); Voltkin-death lightning-cloud (render-driven death-watcher). NO protocol bump.
priorities.check_method: S103 P1 DONE (commit c768549, pushed). #8 Voltkin-vs-chewer + the creature-vs-creature foundation. Gates: tsc 0; vitest 1605/1605 (+11 new); build 578.1/750 KiB (171.9 headroom). FOUNDATION: findNearestEnemyCreatureFrom(world,pos,owner,maxRangeSq?,excludeId?) in creatureAI.ts — generic enemy-creature scan (lowest-CreatureId tie-break, squared range-gate, no RNG/wall-clock), the ONE helper (Council MF7) Voltkin + the P2 turret/HELGA defenders all use; + findNearestEnemyCreature(world,creature) wrapper (range = this creature's attackRange, excludes self). Creature.targetCreatureId added (host-only): default null in makeCreature; save round-trips emit-when-non-null + trimMirrorCreature strips it from the wire (the client never runs the AI -> rehydrates null) so P1 adds ZERO wire surface + needs NO protocol bump (MF6). #8 ORCHESTRATION (MF3 — bonds primary, creatures opportunistic): main.ts SEEKING fan-out sets targetCreatureId = findNearestEnemyCreature ONLY for Voltkin (chewers never), so a Voltkin zaps an enemy chewer only when one is ALREADY within its 180px attackRange — it never navigates toward a distant chewer (targetPos still follows the bond). FSM (creatureLifecycle): SEEKING->ATTACKING now also enters on targetCreatureId!=null; the Voltkin ATTACKING->SEEKING wind-up-abort now requires BOTH targets invalid (bondValid||creatureValid) so a creature-only attack doesn't bounce before firing; targetCreatureId cleared alongside targetBondId on every transition. Attack-fire: creature-FIRST — applyCreatureAttack gained ONE creature-target branch -> damageCreature(targetId, CREATURE_HIT_DAMAGE=1) (chewer hp1 dies in 1; Voltkin keeps its ARC_FLASH zap + killCount++). LIGHTNING-CLOUD (the 'FIRST' foundation the owner asked for): a render-driven death-watcher in creatureRenderer diffs prev/curr world.creatures (the proven S102 chewer-goo pattern, MF2 zero-wire) and pops a cyan electric burst (jagged bolts + glow + playZapBurstSFX procedural) when a Voltkin vanishes from a LIVE state (a kill via damageCreature) — distinguished from a natural lifetime despawn (which passes through DESPAWNING ~60 ticks = ~10 snapshots, reliably observed). DORMANT in P1 (raid is chewer-only; nothing kills a Voltkin yet) — it goes live when P3 laser / P4 HELGA kill Voltkins (a 2-hit Voltkin -> cloud). DETERMINISM (MF4 — gate stays GREEN, never re-baselined): all 1594 prior tests unchanged + green (Voltkin-only replay byte-identical by construction — findNearestEnemyCreature returns null with no enemy creatures so the path is identical); NEW runVoltkinVsChewerStress two-seed byte-identical HARD gate + a combat-sanity assertion (the Voltkin actually kills chewers, killCount>0) + canary. + creatureAI helper tests (enemy-only, range-gate, lowest-id tie-break, excludeId, opportunistic-in-range) + save round-trip/wire-strip tests for targetCreatureId. EVIDENCE-BASED PARTIAL OVERRULE logged: both Council reviewers flagged the death-watcher discriminator as 10Hz-unreliable; overruled with the DESPAWNING-window evidence (kept the proven zero-wire render-diff; fallback synced recentDeaths[] queue logged in carry_forward if playtest shows misses). NOTE: live WebGL screenshots time out on this Pixi rAF game (harness limit) -> verified via unit + determinism tests; cloud/zap are tunable-by-ear procedural synths (owner playtest).
priorities.objective: Land #8 on the S102 damageCreature/HP foundation with MF3 (bonds-primary, creatures-opportunistic) + MF2 (render-driven death VFX) + MF4 (determinism gate stays green). findNearestEnemyCreatureFrom(world,pos,owner,maxRangeSq?) generic helper (no RNG, lowest-CreatureId tie-break) + a creature wrapper. Creature.targetCreatureId host-only (stripped). Voltkin tick: if an enemy creature is within attackRange, zap it this tick; else pursue nearest enemy bond (never navigate toward a distant creature). One creature-target branch in applyCreatureAttack -> damageCreature(target, CREATURE_HIT_DAMAGE=1). Voltkin death-by-kill -> lightning-cloud via creatureRenderer death-watcher (fires on a voltkin that vanished from a LIVE state, not DESPAWNING).
priorities.scope: src/state/creatures/creatureAI.ts (findNearestEnemyCreatureFrom + wrapper + Voltkin opportunistic selection); src/state/creatures/creature.ts (targetCreatureId host-only field + makeCreature default); src/state/creatures/creatureAttack.ts (creature-target branch); src/constants.ts (CREATURE_HIT_DAMAGE=1); src/main.ts (Voltkin target selection in creature-tick); src/state/save.ts (targetCreatureId emit-when-set + trimMirrorCreature strip); src/render/creatureRenderer.ts (Voltkin death-watcher -> lightning-cloud); src/render/effects/* (lightning-cloud) + src/render/audioManager.ts (playZapBurstSFX). Tests: findNearest determinism, Voltkin opportunistic-in-range, kills a chewer (hp1), bond path intact, save round-trip, runCreatureStress Voltkin-vs-chewer (2-seed).
priorities.testing: tsc 0; full vitest green; findNearestEnemyCreatureFrom lowest-id + range-gate determinism; Voltkin zaps a chewer in range but does NOT path toward a distant one (bonds primary); chewer dies in 1 (hp1); creature branch doesn't break the bond sever path; targetCreatureId round-trips (host-only, stripped on wire); Voltkin-ONLY replay byte-identical (no enemy creature -> null path); new Voltkin-vs-chewer golden 2-seed byte-identical; build under 750.

priorities.label: S103 P2 - Generic Defender substrate + recipe generalization: defenders Map + nextDefenderId; defenderLifecycle (register/remove/tick/recipeStillSatisfied/teardown); teardown sites; save additive-optional + WorldSnapshot.defenders (sorted-by-id, ALL fields synced); 3rd 'defender' recipe kind + runDefenderIgnition pass; generic fire/slap poll in main.ts; PROTOCOL 11->12; runDefenderStress HARD gate (incl save/load insta-fire MF5). Defenders removed by recipe-break; Defender.hp sentinel.
priorities.check_method: S103 P2 DONE (commits 3650e64 substrate + fe6ab29 CHECK-fix, pushed). The generic Defender substrate #9/#10 stand on. Gates: tsc 0; vitest 1619/1619 (+14 over P1: 11 substrate/protocol + 3 CHECK-fix); build 583.1/750 KiB (166.9 headroom). BUILT: defenders Map<DefenderId,Defender> + nextDefenderId (worldTypes/world/makeWorld); defenders/{defender.ts (Defender type + DefenderKind 'turret'|'princess' + per-kind DefenderConfig + makeDefender), defenderLifecycle.ts (applyRegisterDefender de-dup-per-anchor / applyRemoveDefender / applyDefenderTick FSM / recipeStillSatisfied via registry stillValid / loadRephaseDefenders MF5 / teardownDefenders)}. FSM IDLE->WINDUP->FIRE->RECOVER: acquire nearest enemy creature via the shared findNearestEnemyCreatureFrom (P1), damage at FIRE entry via unified damageCreature(1), FIRE held DEFENDER_FIRE_HOLD_TICKS=12 (>=2 snapshot intervals so the 1v1 client renders the strike off the synced state — MF1), lastStrikePos captured at fire so the beam has a fixed endpoint after the victim dies, pos pinned to the anchor primitive each tick. RECIPE REGISTRY: added a 3rd 'defender' kind (DefenderGodlyRecipe with defenderKind + stillValid re-validation + DefenderRecipePredicate that skips already-live anchors) + getDefenderRecipe + findDefenderMatches; runDefenderIgnition (godlyOrchestration, mirrors runSpawnerIgnition) registers one defender per recipe per topology-change frame. SAVE: SerializedDefender full round-trip (sorted-by-id MF6; targetCreatureId/lastStrikePos additive-optional; nextDefenderId advance; loadRephaseDefenders prevents insta-fire-on-load MF5). PROTOCOL 11->12 (defenders[] snapshot field + 3 host-internal actions REGISTER/REMOVE/DEFENDER_TICK in KNOWN_GAME_ACTION_TYPES_RECORD, deliberately ABSENT from CLIENT_INTENT_TYPES — defenders auto-build from geometry). Removal = recipe-break (main.ts host poll: throttled per-defender revalidate world.tick%30===id%30 -> REMOVE_DEFENDER if anchor gone / !recipeStillSatisfied; DEFENDER_HP is a high sentinel for a future direct-attack lever, MF8). Teardown wired to all 5 sites (gameMode START+RETURN inline, godlyActions ABORT inline, gameState softReset, world.ts WIN via teardownDefenders). DETERMINISM: runDefenderStress two-seed byte-identical HARD gate (turret+princess co-resident + enemy chewers) + save->load-no-insta-fire + combat sanity (princess kills chewers); defenderLifecycle unit tests. CHECK (FULL Triumvirate: RALPH + Grok-ANALYST + Gemini-AUDITOR on the P1+P2 diff): both FIX-THEN-SHIP. ACCEPTED 1 (Grok: Voltkin wastes a cycle if its opportunistic creature target dies mid-windup -> applyCreatureTick now re-validates targetCreatureId each ATTACKING tick, clears gone/out-of-range/non-enemy so the fire falls back to the bond; +3 tests). PRIME-AUDIT REJECTED 4 with evidence: Gemini-CRIT 'P1 zap invisible' FALSE (ATTACKING lasts 60 ticks=10 snapshots; reuses the shipped synced charge->zap animation + goo death-watcher); Gemini-HIGH 'mid-FIRE load stuck' FALSE (FIRE/RECOVER exit on ticksInState not nextFireTick); Gemini-MED 'lastStrikePos bandwidth' FALSE (already additive-optional emit-when-non-null); Grok-MED '30-tick revalidation delay' = parity with the shipped spawner REVALIDATE_INTERVAL. NOTE: defenders are DORMANT in-game until P3/P4 register the laserTurret + helga recipes (no defender recipe is registered in P2) — the substrate is fully built + tested. Deferred (logged): registering the orphaned PENTAGRAM_RECIPE (orthogonal cleanup w/ potential Codex side-effects).
priorities.objective: Build ONE generic deterministic multiplayer-safe Defender substrate that #9 + #10 stand on (foundations-first). MF1 (FSM state held ~12 ticks + synced is the VFX event bus), MF5 (insta-fire-on-load re-phase), MF6 (sorted-id serialize, all fields synced, protocol 11->12), MF7 (generalize: shared findNearestEnemyCreatureFrom + ignition helper + register the orphaned PENTAGRAM_RECIPE), MF8 (Defender.hp + damageDefender stub + expanded runDefenderStress).
priorities.scope: src/state/worldTypes.ts (defenders Map + nextDefenderId); src/state/world.ts (makeWorld init); src/state/defenders/* NEW (defender type + defenderLifecycle: applyRegisterDefender/applyRemoveDefender/applyDefenderTick FSM/applyDefenderAttack/recipeStillSatisfied/teardownDefenders/loadRephaseDefenders); src/state/gameMode.ts + src/state/godlyActions.ts + src/state/world.ts (4 teardown sites); src/state/godlyRecipes/{index.ts,pentagram.ts,types.ts} (register PENTAGRAM_RECIPE + defender-recipe registry + componentMatchesTypeSet); src/state/godlyOrchestration.ts (runDefenderIgnition shared helper); src/main.ts (generic fire/slap poll); src/state/save.ts (SerializedDefender + WorldSnapshot.defenders sorted-id additive-optional + nextDefenderId advance + load re-phase); src/net/protocol.ts (PROTOCOL 11->12 + REGISTER/REMOVE/TICK/ATTACK_DEFENDER internal actions); src/constants.ts; save.replay.test.ts (runDefenderStress) + protocol test.
priorities.testing: tsc 0; full vitest green; defender registers from geometry; recipeStillSatisfied; FSM tick determinism; runDefenderStress 2-seed byte-identical (HARD gate) incl save->load no-insta-fire (MF5) + co-resident defenders + recipe-break mid-fight + unordered combo + Voltkin target-switch; save round-trip (damaged + targeting defender + nextDefenderId advance, sorted-id); protocol test (defenders internal, version=12); teardown x4 clears defenders+nextDefenderId; build under 750.

priorities.label: S103 P3 - #9 Laser turret on the substrate: recipe 1 Line(deg7)+7 Spiral 'Whip' leaves (strict); fire every TURRET_FIRE_INTERVAL_TICKS=1800; 5 windup rings DERIVED from synced nextFireTick; LASER_BEAM VFX rendered off the synced FIRE state (MF1); kills nearest enemy creature via damageCreature (chewer->goo 1, Voltkin->cloud 2). Original procedural turret art; TurretRenderer.
priorities.check_method: S103 P3 DONE (commit 712c2e9, pushed). #9 laser turret, the first DEFENDER consumer on the P2 substrate. Gates: tsc 0; vitest 1628/1628 (+9: recipe accept/reject matrix + buildable-anchor skip-live + end-to-end ignition->fire->kill); build 588.0/750 KiB (162.0 headroom). RECIPE (godlyRecipes/laserTurret.ts): a STRICT 1-Line(deg7) + 7-Spiral-leaf star, componentOf-isolated exactly like the pentagram — isLaserTurretComponent checks the Line hub has bonds.size===7, the component is exactly 8 prims (hub + 7 leaves), every non-hub is a Spiral of degree 1 (pure leaf) bonded back to the hub, and each Line-Spiral bond is the 'Whip' combo (lookupCombo). Rejects 6/8 leaves, a non-Spiral leaf, a leaf bonded elsewhere, an extra attached shape. DefenderRecipePredicate finds the lowest-id buildable Line that ISN'T already a live defender (reads world.defenders), owner via placerColor (rainbow-safe fallback). registerRecipe(LASER_TURRET_RECIPE) at module tail + side-effect import in main.ts so runDefenderIgnition (findDefenderMatches) + recipeStillSatisfied (getDefenderRecipe.stillValid) both find it. FIRING is the P2 substrate FSM (no new sim) — fires every TURRET_FIRE_INTERVAL_TICKS=1800, kills nearest enemy creature in TURRET_ATTACK_RANGE=420 via the unified damageCreature (chewer->goo via the chewer death-watcher; a Voltkin would take 2->lightning-cloud via the creatureRenderer death-watcher). RENDER (render/turretRenderer.ts, render-only, aboveFogLayer): an ORIGINAL pencil turret (tripod legs + paper-fill housing + an aiming barrel toward the target); the lens CHARGES (cool-blue->hot-red lerp) + up to TURRET_WINDUP_RINGS=5 rings contract toward it — ALL derived from the synced nextFireTick-world.tick (MF1, identical on host + 1v1 client); a thick red jittery BEAM (quadratic curve + white-hot core + muzzle/impact bursts) to the synced lastStrikePos while state==='FIRE' (a fixed endpoint that survives the victim's death); playLaserSFX (procedural square-wave pew + sizzle) on the FIRE edge (per-id state cache). Wired create/sync/clear in main.ts. DETERMINISM: the recipe predicate is pure (sorted-id + componentOf); firing is the P2 determinism-gated FSM (runDefenderStress co-resident turret); the renderer adds zero sim surface. NOTE: live WebGL screenshots time out (Pixi rAF harness limit) -> verified via unit + end-to-end tests; the beam/charge/laser-SFX are tunable-by-ear / by-eye (owner playtest). CHECK: RALPH:PATROL inline (recipe strict + isolated, registration confirmed by the e2e test, render non-determinism, bundle +5 KiB); a combined Council Triumvirate CHECK runs after P4 on the P3+P4 consumer diff.
priorities.objective: First consumer on the Defender substrate. Strict 1-Line-deg7 + 7-Spiral-Whip recipe. Slow heavy turret: fire every 1800 ticks; 5 windup rings derived from the synced nextFireTick-world.tick (client-visible); a visible LASER_BEAM rendered whenever the client observes the synced FIRE state (held ~12 ticks, MF1) drawn to the synced target's pos; kills nearest enemy creature in range via damageCreature (chewer->goo, Voltkin->cloud).
priorities.scope: src/state/godlyRecipes/laserTurret.ts NEW (registerRecipe) + side-effect import in main.ts; src/constants.ts (TURRET_FIRE_INTERVAL_TICKS=1800, TURRET_WINDUP_RINGS=5, TURRET_ATTACK_RANGE~420, TURRET_HP); src/state/defenders/* (turret FSM branch); src/render/turretRenderer.ts NEW (clone ChewerRenderer Graphics; original pencil turret; charge glow keyed to nextFireTick) + main.ts wiring; src/render/effects/laserBeam.ts NEW + effectsRenderer + lifetime; src/render/audioManager.ts (playLaserSFX). Tests: strict recipe (reject 6/8 spirals or non-Whip), fire cadence, target determinism, kill->goo/cloud, windup from nextFireTick.
priorities.testing: tsc 0; full vitest green; strict laser recipe predicate; fire cadence 1800; target determinism (lowest-id); chewer->goo 1 hit / Voltkin->cloud 2 hits; windup rings derive from synced nextFireTick; beam renders off synced FIRE state (visible to 1v1 joiner); runDefenderStress covers turret; build under 750; deploy SUCCESS at milestone.

priorities.label: S103 P4 - #10 HELGA princess on the substrate: recipe central Triangle hub + 3 Spiral 'Warped Anchor' + 3 Circle 'Star' (unordered {Triangle,Circle} type-set, single component). Stationary; slaps nearest enemy CREATURE only (never structures), chewer 1/Voltkin 2. HELGA = ARTICULATED PIXI PUPPET RIG (multi-part hand-authored per-state poses, pose=f(state,ticksInState) pure+deterministic+zero-bundle, thick CtCD-style ORIGINAL art). PrincessRenderer + pure helgaPose(). SLAP VFX off synced SLAP state (MF1).
priorities.check_method: S103 P4 DONE (commit 56adeda HELGA + a29b272 CHECK-fix, pushed; deploy 28155634556 SUCCESS, CHECK-fix deploy in-flight). #10 HELGA, the second consumer + the owner-critical articulated character. Gates: tsc 0; vitest 1645/1645 (+17 over P3: recipe matrix + direction-agnostic-Star + inter-leaf tolerance + e2e ignition->slap->kill + helgaPose determinism/no-NaN/arc-distinctness/offset-desync); build 594.3/750 KiB (155.8 headroom). RECIPE (godlyRecipes/princessHelga.ts): a Triangle hub(deg6) + 3 Spiral leaves (Warped Anchor) + 3 Circle leaves (Star), componentOf size 7, 3+3 leaf-type counts. Star detected by the UNORDERED {Triangle,Circle} type-set (a Circle leaf) — DIRECTION-AGNOSTIC per OC3 (a player who builds Triangle->Circle = lookupCombo 'Wheel' still counts). registerRecipe at tail + side-effect import. HELGA stands/slaps from the Triangle hub; firing is the P2 substrate FSM (slaps nearest enemy CREATURE in PRINCESS_SLAP_RANGE=160 every PRINCESS_SLAP_INTERVAL_TICKS=90 via damageCreature; never structures). RIG (render/helgaPose.ts, PURE + deterministic + unit-tested): a multi-part puppet (skirt/torso/head/beer-arm+stein/slap-arm/2 legs) with HAND-AUTHORED per-FSM-state keyframes — IDLE breathing + a periodic beer-sip; WINDUP the slap-arm coils back + torso leans back (anticipation); FIRE the arm SNAPS across the front + the hand thrusts out (slapReach) + a lunge + open-mouth shout (impact at ticksInState 0); RECOVER eases home. A REAL windup->impact->recover arc (the owner bar), NOT a single-sprite transform-twitch (S96 rejection) NOR a gif. RENDER (render/princessRenderer.ts): draws the rig in ORIGINAL CtCD-style thick graphite outlines + saturated flats (Bavarian dirndl + pewter stein + golden braids VIBE — original character, no franchise likeness; Gemini IP-risk LOW); facing flips toward the target; a slap-impact star-burst at the synced lastStrikePos + playSlapSFX on the FIRE edge — all off SYNCED state (MF1). Wired create/sync/clear in main.ts. CHECK (FULL Triumvirate on the P3+P4 consumer diff): both FIX-THEN-SHIP. ACCEPTED 2: (1) BOTH recipes loosened to tolerate inter-leaf AUTO_BONDs (the pure-leaf degree-1 requirement silently failed to ignite dense stars — worst for the 7-leaf turret; now hub-type+degree+component-size+leaf-type-counts force the star by pigeonhole while a real mismatch still rejects); (2) HELGA idle ambient desynced across instances via a per-defender offset (the synced defenderId) so two HELGAs don't breathe/sip in robotic unison (the owner 'real character' bar). PRIME-AUDIT REJECTED 3: FIRE-edge SFX is reliable (12-tick FIRE >= 2 snapshots, Gemini's own P2 ruling; cache IS pruned); impact-at-lastStrikePos = the owner-accepted S102 green-goo pattern (a DYING-state delay is an unwarranted sim change, logged as polish); builder-feedback-UI = the already-logged pentagram-hint carry. NOTE: live WebGL screenshots time out (Pixi rAF harness limit) -> verified via unit + e2e + pose determinism tests; HELGA's look + the slap/beer SFX are tunable by-eye/by-ear (owner playtest). Generated-art was NOT used (the puppet rig is zero-API/zero-bundle by Council lock) — generative_cost_spent_session stays $0.
priorities.objective: Second consumer + the articulated character. Recipe: a Triangle hub (6 bonds) with neighbors = exactly 3 Spirals (Triangle/Spiral='Warped Anchor') + 3 Circles (unordered {Triangle,Circle}='Star'), single connected component. Stationary defender; FSM IDLE/SIP->ACQUIRE->WINDUP->SLAP (fire tick -> damageCreature on targetCreatureId)->RECOVER; creatures only, never structures; chewer 1 / Voltkin 2. HELGA = a REAL state-driven articulated character via a Pixi puppet rig (Council-locked, DD6): parts skirt/torso/head/beer-arm/slap-arm/legs; helgaPose(state,ticksInState) pure fn (per-part transforms per state, interpolated; genuine windup->impact->recover slap arc; idle breathing + beer-sip); thick painterly CtCD-STYLE outlines; ORIGINAL character (no Peach, no CtCD copy). SLAP VFX (SLAP_IMPACT star-burst) rendered off the synced SLAP state (MF1).
priorities.scope: src/state/godlyRecipes/princessHelga.ts NEW (registerRecipe + componentMatchesTypeSet) + side-effect import; src/constants.ts (PRINCESS_SLAP_INTERVAL_TICKS=90, PRINCESS_SLAP_RANGE~160, PRINCESS_WINDUP_TICKS, PRINCESS_HP); src/state/defenders/* (princess FSM branch incl SIP); src/render/princessRenderer.ts NEW + src/render/helgaPose.ts NEW (pure pose fn) + main.ts wiring; src/render/effects/slapImpact.ts NEW + effectsRenderer; src/render/audioManager.ts (playSlapSFX); memory helga-princess-spec update. Optional: imagen original face texture (additive, never blocks). Tests: recipe predicate (3 WarpedAnchor+3 Star single component; reject Wheel-direction + wrong mix), helgaPose pure-determinism (state x ticks sweep), slap cadence + kill, never targets structures.
priorities.testing: tsc 0; full vitest green; HELGA recipe predicate (3 Warped Anchor + 3 Star single component; reject Wheel-dir confusion + wrong neighbor mix); helgaPose pure (same state,ticks -> same pose, full sweep); slap cadence + kills nearest enemy creature; never targets a structure/bond; SLAP VFX off synced state (1v1 joiner sees it); runDefenderStress covers princess; build under 750; deploy SUCCESS; boot-then-smoke live (HELGA idles/sips, winds up, slaps a chewer dead).

priorities.label: S103 P5 (budget-gated GREEN stretch) - Tier-1 G4 build-feel juice: bond-formation juice burst (render-local, synced-tick deterministic) + in-world leader crown over the top-score seat. Pure render; additive-optional if any wire. Skipped -> carry-forward if budget <= YELLOW after P4.
priorities.deferral_reason: Budget gate fired as designed: context 598.8K/1M (59.9% YELLOW) after P4 — P5 was explicitly GREEN-gated in the PDR. Deferred to a future session (Tier-1 G4 build-feel juice). The TD defenders bundle (#8/#9/#10 + substrate), the user's primary ask, shipped complete.
priorities.objective: If budget stays GREEN after P4, ship the highest value-per-risk Tier-1 item (G4 juice: bond-formation burst + leader crown) - pure render/feel, no LOCKED-Section-6 gate. Otherwise log as carry-forward.
priorities.scope: src/render/* bond-formation burst hook (on new-bond render-tick) + in-world leader crown over top-score seat; deterministic synced-tick; additive-optional if any wire surface.
priorities.testing: tsc 0; full vitest green; render-local determinism; (if wired) additive-optional round-trip; build under 750. BUDGET-GATED: execute only if real-context < 750K (GREEN/low-YELLOW) after P4.

reflexion_entries_to_archive: S103 P1 #the-opportunistic-override-stays-byte-identical-when-the-population-is-empty: #8 (Voltkin attacks chewers) looked like it would disturb Voltkin's locked replay, but framing it as an OPPORTUNISTIC override (Council MF3 — bonds drive navigation, a creature is only zapped when ALREADY in attackRange) made every new branch reduce to the pre-existing expression when no enemy creatures exist: the new findNearestEnemyCreature returns null -> targetCreatureId stays null -> the FSM SEEKING->ATTACKING add (`|| targetCreatureId!==null`), the wind-up-abort change (`!bondValid && !creatureValid` vs the old `!bondValid`), and the attack-fire guard all collapse to identical behavior. Result: ALL 1594 prior tests stayed green with NO re-baseline (resolving Grok's 're-baseline violates the HARD gate' objection precisely — the two-seed determinism gate never moved; only NEW chewer-present scenarios got new golden coverage). LESSON: to add a behavior to a determinism-locked entity, gate the new code on a condition that is provably absent in the existing test corpus (here: enemy creatures), then the old replays are byte-identical BY CONSTRUCTION and you add fresh determinism coverage for the new path instead of mutating the baseline. Also: building the lightning-cloud as a render-driven prev/curr-diff death-watcher (the proven S102 chewer-goo zero-wire pattern) let me ship the 'lightning-cloud FIRST' foundation in P1 even though nothing kills a Voltkin yet (it's dormant until P3/P4) — and I overruled BOTH Council reviewers' 10Hz-unreliability flag with specific evidence (DESPAWNING lasts ~60 ticks = ~10 snapshots, so a kill-from-a-live-state is a reliable discriminator), logging the synced-recentDeaths[] fallback rather than preemptively regressing a proven pattern.

S103 P2 #mirror-the-spawner-substrate-then-the-recipe-registry-already-has-the-seam: the generic Defender substrate (a whole new synced entity + protocol bump) was far lower-risk than it looked because S100's spawner is a complete, battle-tested template — entity leaf type + lifecycle reducers + Map<Id,Entity>+nextId + 5 teardown sites + additive-optional sorted-id save + host-internal-not-client-intent protocol rows. Cloning that SHAPE (not the behavior) for defenders got the determinism/wire/save/teardown all correct on the first typecheck-green pass. The recipe registry ALSO already had the seam: a 'cinematic'|'spawner' kind discriminator + findSpawnerMatch, so adding a 3rd 'defender' kind + findDefenderMatches + runDefenderIgnition slotted in cleanly. KEY DETERMINISM/WIRE PATTERN (Council MF1): a one-shot strike VFX on a PERSISTING entity can't be a render-diff (the entity doesn't vanish) AND can't be a raw-cadence derivation (10Hz misses the exact tick) — so the FSM FIRE state is HELD ~12 ticks (>=2 snapshot intervals) + SYNCED = the state IS the event bus; + a synced lastStrikePos captured at fire so the beam keeps its endpoint after a one-shot kill removes the victim. CHECK PRIME-AUDIT lesson: the two Council auditors produced 5 findings, 4 of them FALSE-POSITIVES from not knowing two specific durations — (a) Voltkin ATTACKING lasts 60 ticks (so the 'zap invisible at 10Hz' CRIT was wrong; it reuses the shipped Voltkin-vs-bond animation), and (b) FIRE/RECOVER exit on ticksInState not nextFireTick (so the 'mid-FIRE load stuck' HIGH was wrong). The ONE real finding (a Voltkin wasting a cycle on a creature target that died mid-windup) was a genuine cheap robustness fix. Reviewers reason from generic priors; PRIME-AUDIT against the actual FSM-state DURATIONS is what separates the real from the hallucinated. MF5 insta-fire-on-load (re-phase nextFireTick to tick + ((next-tick)%interval+interval)%interval) is the despawnAtTick=0 bug class — always re-phase any saved absolute-tick cadence field on load.

S103 P3 #a-consumer-on-a-good-substrate-is-just-a-recipe-plus-a-renderer: with the P2 Defender substrate (FSM + firing + save + protocol + determinism gate) already built + tested, shipping #9 (laser turret) was ONLY (a) a recipe predicate + (b) a render — zero new sim code, zero new determinism risk. The recipe reuses the pentagram's componentOf-isolation idiom verbatim (a Line hub deg-7 + 7 degree-1 Spiral leaves, component size exactly 8) so it's strict + spacing-sensitive by the SAME accepted contract; the predicate reads world.defenders to skip already-live anchors (so build-one-per-frame + rebuild-after-removal both fall out for free). The recipe MUST registerRecipe at module tail (pentagram is the cautionary counter-example: it's imported as a side-effect but never registers, so it relies on a hardcoded findAllPentagramAnchors — the defender path instead goes through the generic findDefenderMatches/getDefenderRecipe registry, which only works if registered). All the wire-split hard-thinking was already paid down in P2 (FIRE-state-held + synced lastStrikePos), so the turret renderer just READS synced state: charge + wind-up rings from nextFireTick, beam from state==='FIRE' to lastStrikePos, SFX on the FIRE edge — identical on host + client with zero new wire surface. LESSON: invest the determinism/wire/protocol thinking ONCE in the substrate; then each consumer is a cheap, low-risk recipe + renderer.

S103 P4 #a-puppet-rig-IS-the-real-character-the-owner-asked-for-and-an-articulated-rig-beats-veo-for-an-action-character: HELGA (the owner-critical 'real state-driven character, NOT a gif/twitch') shipped as a PURE Pixi puppet rig — helgaPose(state,ticksInState,phaseTick,offset) returns per-part transforms for a multi-limb body (skirt/torso/head/beer-arm/slap-arm/legs) with HAND-AUTHORED keyframes per FSM state. The S96 'PowerPoint spin' rejection was about transform-twitching ONE flat sprite; an articulated rig that rotates INDEPENDENT limbs through authored windup->impact->recover angles is a categorically different, owner-sanctioned thing (OC3 explicitly allowed 'a rig/puppet OR per-state hand-authored animation'). Choosing the rig over veo-generated frames (a documented prior owner preference) was the right call for an ACTION character with discrete game states + an autonomous run: deterministic by construction (pose=f(state,ticks) → replay-safe + unit-testable: I asserted the windup-arm-goes-back, fire-arm-snaps-across-with-reach, recover-eases-home arc + a no-NaN sweep), zero-bundle, zero-API-dependency (no veo/imagen key flakiness in the critical path), and crisper state-driven action than a video loop. KEY CHECK CATCH (Gemini, accepted): a pure pose fn driven by the GLOBAL phaseTick makes N instances animate in robotic UNISON — add a per-entity offset (the synced defenderId) to desync the idle ambient; that single change is what makes a rig read 'alive' rather than 'a row of clones'. RECIPE lesson: match the owner's combo SEMANTICS not the lookupCombo direction — 'Star' = the unordered {Triangle,Circle} type-set (a Circle leaf), so I check leaf TYPE not lookupCombo (which would call a Triangle->Circle build 'Wheel' and wrongly reject it). And (Council, both): a 'pure leaf = degree-1' recipe gate silently fails to ignite when dense AUTO_BOND bonds adjacent leaves — gate on hub-degree + component-size + leaf-type-COUNTS (which force the star by pigeonhole) and TOLERATE inter-leaf bonds; strictness via the size/type guard, not via per-leaf degree.

PLAYTEST S103 LIVE: build a Line+7-Spirals -> a pencil laser turret charges (5 rings) + beams the nearest chewer every 30s; build a Triangle+3-Spiral+3-Circle -> HELGA idles/sips a stein, winds up + SLAPS nearby chewers (yey the articulated rig — tune her look + the laser/slap SFX by eye/ear); summon a Voltkin near enemy chewers -> it zaps one that wanders within 180px while still marching on the base (a 2-hit Voltkin death = lightning-cloud). Retune any synth/VFX by feel.

Generated-art enhancement (optional): HELGA shipped as a deterministic Pixi PUPPET RIG (zero-API). If the owner wants painterly texture, layer an imagen-generated original face/texture onto the head part (additive, must stay replay-safe). See memory helga-princess-spec.
TD connector visible-damage + multi-chewer stacking (S102 de-scoped polish): a real Bond.hp + damaged-bond render (current model is per-creature chewProgress; CONNECTOR_HP=CHEW_HITS).

Death VFX fallback (S103 MF2 watch-item): if playtest shows missed lightning-clouds/goo on the 1v1 client, add a bounded synced recentDeaths[] queue (Gemini's design) instead of the render-driven prev/curr diff.

TD recipe-build UX (S101+S103 CHECK, LOW): defender/spawner recipes are spacing-sensitive + give NO feedback on a near-miss — add an in-build affordance (highlight an invalid leaf / 'connect to the hub' hint / closing-edge ghost). The S103 CHECK loosened the recipes to tolerate inter-leaf auto-bonds, which helps, but the unguided-build gap remains.

Defender polish (S103 CHECK, logged-not-blocking): a slapped/beamed creature vanishes the same tick (impact VFX shows at lastStrikePos over empty space) — same accepted S102 green-goo tradeoff; if it feels disconnected in playtest, add a short DYING creature state. Also: register the orphaned PENTAGRAM_RECIPE (inert cleanup, watch Codex side-effects).

Tier-1 roadmap remainder: G1b MOTION (Wheel/Star rotation, Council-deferred - needs a mechanical verb), G2 family traits (needs LOCKED_DECISIONS Section 6 lock-amendment), G3b Codex silhouettes, G4 juice (= P5 above).
